metadata.description: Free, production-tested Claude Code skills. safe-multi-session-deploy: stop concurrent AI agents / Claude Code sessions from clobbering work, deploying to the wrong Vercel project, or shipping blind. visual-iteration: let an AI see what it builds — render a frame, compare to references, and iterate web design or 3D/game scenes until they look right.

plugins.name: safe-multi-session-deploy
plugins.description: Pre-deploy safety for any project shared by multiple AI agents or sessions, pushing to ANY live host. Fixes: a deploy 'succeeds' but the live site never updates (wrong target/environment/project), parallel agents clobbering each other's uncommitted work, and not knowing which session's build is actually live. Host-agnostic — Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare, Railway, Render, Fly, AWS/S3, a VPS over SSH/rsync, Docker, or FTP — via a pluggable deploy command. Stamps and verifies a session-tagged build-provenance marker on the live artifact.

plugins.name: visual-iteration
plugins.description: Let an AI SEE what it builds and fix it. Render -> look -> compare-to-reference -> edit -> re-render loop for getting web design (layout, CSS, spacing, typography, color) or 3D/game scenes (character look, mesh, texture, placement, lighting, camera framing) visually correct. Deterministically renders one frame via headless Chrome + Playwright, reads the image, compares to reference images or a style brief, and iterates. Includes a real-GPU-over-CDP path so WebGL post-processing (bloom/SSAO/tonemapping) matches what users see, not SwiftShader. For Three.js, react-three-fiber, raw WebGL, canvas, and any web framework.
